14, 15 and 16 under the Fundamental Rights Chapter, and forms 
part of the basic structure of our Constitution. Furthermore, the other 
provisions of the Constitution, more importantly the golden triangle of 
Articles 14, 19 and 21 would take within their sweep every right that 
forms part of the Right to life which certainly and most importantly 
includes the right to live with dignity. 
34. While Article 15 of the Constitution specifically bars the State 
from discriminating against any citizen of India on grounds only of 
religion, race, caste, sex, place of birth, or any of them, the specific 
ground of ‘disability’ is conspicuous by its absence. Though the 
anti-discrimination and non-discrimination clauses under Article 15 
were discussed at length in the Constituent Assembly, ‘disability’ 
as a ground for non-discrimination was not included in Article 15. A
